title: toxicodendron rubifolium
intro: Toxicodendron rubifolium, also known as the Western Poison Ivy, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its shiny green leaves and distinctive red or yellow fall color. The plant contains urushiol, a potent allergen that can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in most people. Despite its toxic nature, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron rubifolium are typically used topically in the form of salves, ointments, or tinctures, and may be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.
